title: geigeria brevifolia
intro: Geigeria brevifolia, also known as the wild marigold or bitter marigold, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to South Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Geigeria brevifolia is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infused in oil for its herbal uses.
